dag_desc: Remove done:true from the output vocabulary. Wire scoring into terminal brief. Make chaining the only exit from a completed DAG. The improvement-6→7→8 loop becomes the default behavior, not the exception.

tasks.desc: Current state: all tests passing, scoring infrastructure (MetricsExtractor, trail-metrics) built but unwired, done:true leaks from advance and orient, chaining mechanism operational but never triggered by default.

Connect trail-metrics scoring to the terminal brief path. Add a 'scoring' field to the TerminalBrief type carrying velocity, batch duration, retry counts, and coverage signals computed from trail.jsonl. Extend Context (src/runtime/context.ts) to load trail metrics via loadContext() so the pure buildTerminalBrief() in src/runtime/brief.ts can include them. Update the IO-based buildTerminalBrief in src/lib/terminal-brief.ts to also compute and include scoring. After this node, any terminal advance output includes scoring data from the session trail.

Remove every code path that emits done:true or complete:true from CLI output. In src/cli/advance.ts: both advanceNode (line ~370) and advanceBatchCmd (line ~451) terminal paths must emit chainReady:true instead of done:true. The message changes from 'All work complete' to guidance about successor spec creation. In src/cli/orient.ts: rename the 'complete' field to 'chainReady' (line ~144) — true means 'all nodes executed, time to chain' not 'time to stop'. When orient detects chainReady, include the existing pendingSpecs scan plus a message directing agents to evaluate and spec. Update any tests that assert on done:true or complete:true in CLI output.

The chainReady output from terminal advance carries actionable content for successor spec authoring. Broaden detectGaps() in src/lib/terminal-audit/detected.ts beyond structural contracts: add scoring-derived gap types — nodes with only artifact-exists validators (no shell coverage), produces with no test evidence in completion records, velocity decay across batches (later batches significantly slower than earlier). The advance terminal output includes: scoring summary, full gap surface (structural + scoring-derived), root intent, iteration count, and concrete 'what to improve next' items derived from gaps. An agent reading this output has everything needed to write a successor spec without additional context.

Integration test proving the asymptotic loop holds. Test 1: create a minimal DAG, advance all nodes to terminal, assert output contains chainReady:true with scoring and gaps — never done:true. Test 2: orient on a fully-advanced DAG returns chainReady:true, not complete:true. Test 3: terminal brief includes scoring data from trail. Test 4: detectGaps returns scoring-derived gaps (no-shell-coverage, untested-evidence) for a DAG with only artifact-exists validators. Test 5: the chainReady output includes enough context to write a successor spec (rootIntent, iteration, gaps, scoring present).

Asymptotic chain loop verified: no CLI command emits done:true, scoring feeds terminal brief, chainReady is the only completion signal, detectGaps includes scoring-derived gaps, agents receive sufficient context to write successor specs. The improvement-6→7→8 pattern is now the default, not the exception.
